2026-04-21T15:56:36.1932624Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-21T15:56:36.1933232Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T15:56:36.1933672Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T15:56:36.1934041Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T15:56:36.1934356Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T15:56:36.1934598Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T15:56:36.1934754Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-21T15:56:36.1934937Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T15:56:36.1935107Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-21T15:56:36.1935348Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-21T15:56:36.1936108Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy...

This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

2026-04-21T15:59:40.0162999Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T15:57:53.426106709Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58449,\"title\":\"[Search] Docs: Add KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient documentation to README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Search.Documents`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 10b1ccf3e (PR #58411) by `@jorgerangel-msft`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe push updates the TypeSpec/http-client-csharp emitter version and regenerates SDK code. The affected package `Azure.Search.Documents` 12.0.0 (released 2026-04-14) introduced `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` for agentic retrieval against knowledge bases — a major new feature — but the README has never been updated to document it.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README has **no section** documenting `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` or the Knowledge Base Retrieval feature, despite it being the headline new addition in version 12.0.0.\\n- A complete test-backed sample file already exists at `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` with seven `#region Snippet:` regions ready for inclusion, but none are referenced in the README.\\n- The Examples table-of-contents in the README does not list Knowledge Base Retrieval.\\n- The Key Concepts section has no description of knowledge bases, knowledge sources, or agentic retrieval.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n1. In the **Key concepts** section (after the existing Vector search sub-section, before Thread safety), add a new sub-section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n#### Knowledge base retrieval\\n\\n`KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` provides agentic retrieval operations against Azure AI Search knowledge bases. A knowledge base is a managed collection of knowledge sources (search indexes, Azure Blobs, web content, OneLake) with an associated Azure OpenAI model that can synthesize responses to natural-language intents. Use `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` to query the knowledge base with semantic intents and receive synthesized answers with cited references.\\n```\\n\\n2. In the **Examples** table-of-contents block, add a new bullet after `* [Async APIs](#async-apis)`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n* [Knowledge base retrieval](#knowledge-base-retrieval)\\n```\\n\\n3. After the `### Async APIs` section (and before `### Authenticate in a National Cloud`), add a new section:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Knowledge base retrieval\\n\\n`KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` enables agentic retrieval from an Azure AI Search knowledge base. First, add the required using directives:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Namespaces\\n```\\n\\nThen create a client and issue a retrieval request with a natural-language intent:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Retrieve\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippet tool will populate the placeholder blocks above from the existing test file. No new test code needs to be written — `Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` already contains the required regions.\\n\\n| Snippet name | Test file location | Region start line |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Namespaces` | `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` | 10 |\\n| `Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Retrieve` | `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` | 39 |\\n\\nInsert the placeholder blocks exactly as shown in Step 1 above. `Update-Snippets.ps1` will replace the content between the fences.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all six required top-level sections. After the change, confirm:\\n\\n- `## Key concepts` — now includes the new **Knowledge base retrieval** sub-section\\n- `## Examples` — TOC now links to `#knowledge-base-retrieval`\\n- New `### Knowledge base retrieval` section appears between `### Async APIs` and `### Authenticate in a National Cloud`\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order from the repository root:\\n\\n```powershell\\ndotnet build sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/src/\\ndotnet test sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/ --filter TestCategory!=Live\\neng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 search\\neng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 search\\ndotnet format sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/\\n```\\n\\nAfter running `Update-Snippets.ps1`, verify with `git diff sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/README.md` that the two snippet placeholders were populated with the sample code.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-21T15:59:40.0293557Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T15:58:04.3996205Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58449,\"title\":\"[Search] Docs: Add KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient documentation to README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Search.Documents`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 10b1ccf3e (PR #58411) by `@jorgerangel-msft`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe push updates the TypeSpec/http-client-csharp emitter version and regenerates SDK code. The affected package `Azure.Search.Documents` 12.0.0 (released 2026-04-14) introduced `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` for agentic retrieval against knowledge bases — a major new feature — but the README has never been updated to document it.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README has **no section** documenting `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` or the Knowledge Base Retrieval feature, despite it being the headline new addition in version 12.0.0.\\n- A complete test-backed sample file already exists at `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` with seven `#region Snippet:` regions ready for inclusion, but none are referenced in the README.\\n- The Examples table-of-contents in the README does not list Knowledge Base Retrieval.\\n- The Key Concepts section has no description of knowledge bases, knowledge sources, or agentic retrieval.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n1. In the **Key concepts** section (after the existing Vector search sub-section, before Thread safety), add a new sub-section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n#### Knowledge base retrieval\\n\\n`KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` provides agentic retrieval operations against Azure AI Search knowledge bases. A knowledge base is a managed collection of knowledge sources (search indexes, Azure Blobs, web content, OneLake) with an associated Azure OpenAI model that can synthesize responses to natural-language intents. Use `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` to query the knowledge base with semantic intents and receive synthesized answers with cited references.\\n```\\n\\n2. In the **Examples** table-of-contents block, add a new bullet after `* [Async APIs](#async-apis)`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n* [Knowledge base retrieval](#knowledge-base-retrieval)\\n```\\n\\n3. After the `### Async APIs` section (and before `### Authenticate in a National Cloud`), add a new section:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Knowledge base retrieval\\n\\n`KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` enables agentic retrieval from an Azure AI Search knowledge base. First, add the required using directives:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Namespaces\\n```\\n\\nThen create a client and issue a retrieval request with a natural-language intent:\\n\\n```C# Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Retrieve\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippet tool will populate the placeholder blocks above from the existing test file. No new test code needs to be written — `Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` already contains the required regions.\\n\\n| Snippet name | Test file location | Region start line |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Namespaces` | `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` | 10 |\\n| `Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Retrieve` | `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` | 39 |\\n\\nInsert the placeholder blocks exactly as shown in Step 1 above. `Update-Snippets.ps1` will replace the content between the fences.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all six required top-level sections. After the change, confirm:\\n\\n- `## Key concepts` — now includes the new **Knowledge base retrieval** sub-section\\n- `## Examples` — TOC now links to `#knowledge-base-retrieval`\\n- New `### Knowledge base retrieval` section appears between `### Async APIs` and `### Authenticate in a National Cloud`\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order from the repository root:\\n\\n```powershell\\ndotnet build sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/src/\\ndotnet test sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/ --filter TestCategory!=Live\\neng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 search\\neng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 search\\ndotnet format sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/\\n```\\n\\nAfter running `Update-Snippets.ps1`, verify with `git diff sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/README.md` that the two snippet placeholders were populated with the sample code.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
